title: Health, Health Care, and Aging
text:
Health, Health Care, and Aging

Health, Health Care, and Aging

RAND advances understanding of health and health behaviors, and examines how the organization and financing of care affect costs, quality, and access. Our body of research includes innovative studies of health insurance, health care reform, and health information technology, as well as obesity, substance use disorders, and PTSD. RAND findings also help inform policies that aim to improve the health of seniors and the care they receive.

Research

Jun 29, 2026

Differences in Rural and Urban Home Healthcare Agency Responses to the COVID-19 Pandemic

With COVID-19 impacting urban and rural communities differently, we examined how home healthcare agencies experienced and responded to the pandemic by rurality.

Research

Jun 29, 2026

Medicaid Prior Authorization and Discontinuation of Buprenorphine

This study assesses the duration of buprenorphine episodes paid by fee-for-service and managed care organization plans with and without prior authorization (PA) in Medicaid.

Commentary

Jun 26, 2026

The Texas Screwworm Cases Are a Wake-Up Call for U.S. Biosecurity

The U.S. biosecurity system is poorly prepared for new threats. Naturally occurring crop diseases not yet present could cause billions of dollars in losses if introduced here. Engineered pathogens delivered deliberately could be far worse.

Research

Jun 26, 2026

China's Evolving Global Health Strategy And Its Implications For The US

Examines how China shifts its global health engagement from the Belt and Road Initiative toward the Global Development Initiative as the United States retreats toward an America First approach, weighing what the divergence means for US influence.

Research

Jun 26, 2026

Longitudinal Correlates of Internalized HIV Stigma Among Ugandan PLWH

This study examined longitudinal correlates of internalized HIV stigma among people living with HIV in Uganda.

Research

Jun 25, 2026

Determinants of COVID-19 Vaccine Hesitancy Across 186 Countries: A Multifaceted Analysis Using Structural Equation Modeling

This research examined the prevalence of COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy and its influencing factors.

Research

Jun 24, 2026

Systems That Shape Acculturative Stressors and Health Equity in the Latine Community: A Community-Engaged Systems Mapping Analysis

This study employs a systems thinking approach to explore the dynamic systems influencing acculturative stress and resilience among Latine populations in North Carolina.

Research

Jun 24, 2026

Where State, Market, and Community Meet: Village Doctors and the Governance of Rural Health in China

We examine the unique role of village doctors in shaping public health governance in rural China.

Research

Jun 24, 2026

Alcohol-free and Low-Alcohol Beverage Consumption and Concurrent and Subsequent Alcohol Outcomes Among Young Adults

Found that alcohol-free and low-alcohol beverage use was associated with lower social drinking motives and a higher likelihood of alcohol abstention one year later among young adult drinkers in California.

Research

Jun 23, 2026

Quality and Patient Safety Review Processes in Military Treatment Facilities and Operational Clinical Services

RAND researchers assessed the quality and patient safety review processes for care delivered in military treatment facilities and via operational clinical services. Findings were used to identify potential opportunities for improvement.

Press

Jun 23, 2026

Nearly 5 Million U.S. Veterans Have Used Psilocybin Mushrooms, LSD or MDMA; Veterans' Policy Preferences Differ by Psychedelic Substance

An estimated 4.8 million U.S. veterans have used psilocybin mushrooms, LSD, or MDMA in their lifetime. About half of them are unsure about discussing their use with a VA doctor. Despite recent federal government investments in psychedelics research, VA policy remains unclear.

Research

Jun 23, 2026

U.S. Veterans and Psychedelics: Prevalence of Use and Policy Preferences

In this report, the authors present new data on U.S veterans’ use of specific psychedelic substances (such as psilocybin mushrooms, LSD, and ibogaine), their psychedelics policy preferences, and how these compare with nonveterans.
